heading: Selective early retirement: reserve general and flag officers of the Navy and Marine Corps
label: 14705 Selective early retirement: reserve general and flag officers of the Navy and Marine Corps
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , prior to repeal by .section 6389(f)(3) of this titlePub. L. 103–337, § 1628(4)

Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “Navy Reserve” for “Naval Reserve”.Pub. L. 109–1632003—Subsec. (b)(1). substituted “selection board” for “continuation board”.Pub. L. 108–1361998—Subsec. (b). designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted “of officers” after “consideration” and “continuation” after “shall convene a”, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 105–261

children.heading: .—Authority To Consider
children.label: a .—Authority To Consider
children.text: An officer in the Navy Reserve in an active status serving in the grade of rear admiral (lower half) or rear admiral and an officer in the Marine Corps Reserve in an active status serving in the grade of brigadier general or major general may be considered for early retirement whenever the Secretary of the Navy determines that such action is necessary.

If the Secretary of the Navy determines that consideration of officers for early retirement under this section is necessary, the Secretary shall convene a selection board under to recommend an appropriate number of officers for early retirement.section 14101(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: each regular commissioned officer appointed to the board holds a grade higher than the grade of rear admiral or major general; and

children.label: B
children.text: at least one member of the board is a reserve officer who holds the grade of rear admiral or major general.

children.heading: 14514.—Separation Under Section
children.label: c 14514.—Separation Under Section
children.text: An officer selected for early retirement under this section shall be separated in accordance with .section 14514 of this title
